title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: lstparsR reads NONMEM .lst listing files extracts parameter estimates tidy tibbles. designed population pharmacokinetic pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) workflows many model runs need parsed programmatically. Key design choices: output returned tibbles consistent column names. Missing quantities (e.g., standard errors covariance step absent) NA, never errors. VECTOR blocks (THETA) lower-triangular MATRIX blocks (OMEGA, SIGMA) handled, including multi-line parameter sets.

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: Use read_lst_file() load .lst file S3 object class "lst": lst object simply character vector (one element per line) class "lst" attached. fetch_* functions require class.
code: path <- system.file("testdata", "full_cov.lst", package = "lstparsR") lst  <- read_lst_file(path) lst #> <lst> NONMEM listing file: 1321 lines summary(lst) #> <lst> NONMEM listing file #>   Lines            : 1321 #>   Estimation method: FIRST ORDER CONDITIONAL ESTIMATION WITH INTERACTION #>   Covariance step  : TRUE

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: row corresponds one THETA parameter. se rse columns contain standard error relative standard error (percentage), respectively. Use digits argument round RSE values:

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: fetch_etas() extracts diagonal OMEGA covariance matrix, plus ETA shrinkage reported: Shrinkage values can rounded separately:
code: fetch_etas(lst) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   parameter estimate      se         rse shrinkage #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 ETA1       0.2      0.0389        19.4        NA #> 2 ETA2       0.255    0.0463        18.2        NA #> 3 ETA3       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 4 ETA4       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 5 ETA5       0.00001 68      680000000          NA #> 6 ETA6       0.00001 NA             NA          NA #> 7 ETA7       0.00001 NA             NA          NA fetch_etas(lst, digits = 1, shk_digits = 1) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   parameter estimate      se         rse shrinkage #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 ETA1       0.2      0.0389        19.4        NA #> 2 ETA2       0.255    0.0463        18.2        NA #> 3 ETA3       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 4 ETA4       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 5 ETA5       0.00001 68      680000000          NA #> 6 ETA6       0.00001 NA             NA          NA #> 7 ETA7       0.00001 NA             NA          NA

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
code: fetch_sigmas(lst) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   parameter estimate      se    rse #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 EPS1        0.0769 0.00434   5.64 #> 2 EPS2        0.123  0.199   162.

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: condition number computed ratio largest smallest eigenvalue correlation matrix parameter estimates. Values 1000 suggest numerical instability:

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: fetch_all() runs every parser single call. Individual parser failures caught returned NULL warning, single problematic section abort entire extraction:

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: NONMEM run complete covariance step, SE, RSE, shrinkage, condition number returned NA:

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: common pattern parse many .lst files :
code: library(purrr)  lst_files <- list.files("models/", pattern = "\\.lst$", full.names = TRUE)  results <- map(lst_files, \(f) {   lst <- read_lst_file(f)   fetch_all(lst) })  # Combine all THETA tables all_thetas <- map_dfr(results, "thetas", .id = "run")

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: lstparsR detects supports following NONMEM estimation methods: First Order (FO) First Order Conditional Estimation (FOCE) First Order Conditional Estimation Interaction (FOCE-) Stochastic Approximation Expectation-Maximization (SAEM) Importance Sampling (IMP) Importance Sampling Assisted Mode Posteriori (IMPMAP) Markov Chain Monte Carlo Bayesian Analysis

title: Getting Started with lstparsR
text: interactive use, launch built-Shiny application: opens browser-based interface can upload .lst files, view parsed results tables, download CSV RDS.

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: Population pharmacokinetic pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) modelling NONMEM produces listing files (.lst) contain parameter estimates, standard errors, objective function values, eigenvalues, shrinkage statistics structured complex text format. Extracting quantities downstream analysis — model comparison tables, forest plots, covariate analyses — typically requires manual copy-paste ad-hoc scripts fragile error-prone. lstparsR provides clean, validated interface reading .lst files returning tidy data frames. designed two goals: Robustness. parsers return NA unavailable quantities rather raising errors. makes package safe batch workflows NONMEM runs may failed terminated early. Tidiness. output returned tibbles consistent, lowercase column names (parameter, estimate, se, rse, shrinkage) ready binding across runs dplyr purrr. package supports major NONMEM estimation methods: FOCE-, FOCE, FO, SAEM, IMP, IMPMAP, Bayesian analysis.

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
code: library(lstparsR)  path <- system.file("testdata", "full_cov.lst", package = "lstparsR") lst  <- read_lst_file(path) lst #> <lst> NONMEM listing file: 1321 lines result <- fetch_all(lst) names(result) #> [1] "thetas" "etas"   "sigmas" "ofv"    "condn"

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: read_lst_file() reads .lst file S3 object class "lst". simply character vector (one element per line) class attribute attached. fetch_* functions require class input.
code: lst <- read_lst_file(   system.file("testdata", "full_cov.lst", package = "lstparsR") ) summary(lst) #> <lst> NONMEM listing file #>   Lines            : 1321 #>   Estimation method: FIRST ORDER CONDITIONAL ESTIMATION WITH INTERACTION #>   Covariance step  : TRUE

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_thetas() extracts THETA parameter estimates FINAL PARAMETER ESTIMATE section. covariance step completed, standard errors (SE) relative standard errors (RSE, percent) also reported. digits argument rounds RSE values:

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_etas() extracts diagonal OMEGA covariance matrix (variance inter-individual random effects). ETA shrinkage included reported NONMEM. RSE shrinkage rounding can controlled independently:
code: fetch_etas(lst) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   parameter estimate      se         rse shrinkage #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 ETA1       0.2      0.0389        19.4        NA #> 2 ETA2       0.255    0.0463        18.2        NA #> 3 ETA3       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 4 ETA4       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 5 ETA5       0.00001 68      680000000          NA #> 6 ETA6       0.00001 NA             NA          NA #> 7 ETA7       0.00001 NA             NA          NA fetch_etas(lst, digits = 1, shk_digits = 1) #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   parameter estimate      se         rse shrinkage #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 ETA1       0.2      0.0389        19.4        NA #> 2 ETA2       0.255    0.0463        18.2        NA #> 3 ETA3       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 4 ETA4       0.1     73          73000          NA #> 5 ETA5       0.00001 68      680000000          NA #> 6 ETA6       0.00001 NA             NA          NA #> 7 ETA7       0.00001 NA             NA          NA

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_sigmas() extracts diagonal SIGMA covariance matrix (variance residual error terms).
code: fetch_sigmas(lst) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   parameter estimate      se    rse #>   <chr>        <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 EPS1        0.0769 0.00434   5.64 #> 2 EPS2        0.123  0.199   162.

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_ofv() extracts OFV #OBJV: line. failed early-terminated runs, falls back workflow footer (OFV = ...) present.

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_condn() computes condition number correlation matrix parameter estimates ratio largest smallest eigenvalue. Condition numbers 1000 suggest numerical instability estimation.

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: fetch_all() convenience wrapper calls every parser returns named list. Individual failures caught returned NULL warning, one problematic section abort entire call.

what: Handling Runs Without a Covariance Step
title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: NONMEM complete covariance step, SE, RSE, shrinkage, condition number returned NA. parameter estimates OFV still available.
code: path2 <- system.file("testdata", "theta_no_cov.lst", package = "lstparsR") lst2  <- read_lst_file(path2) summary(lst2) #> <lst> NONMEM listing file #>   Lines            : 1246 #>   Estimation method: FIRST ORDER CONDITIONAL ESTIMATION WITH INTERACTION #>   Covariance step  : TRUE fetch_thetas(lst2) #> # A tibble: 12 × 4 #>    parameter     estimate       se     rse #>    <chr>            <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 TH_1          34.1     3.37e+ 0 9.88e 0 #>  2 TH_2      387000       5.41e+ 7 1.40e 4 #>  3 TH_3           8.05    5.2 e- 1 6.46e 0 #>  4 TH_4           1.78    1.49e- 1 8.37e 0 #>  5 TH_5           0.498   2.78e- 2 5.58e 0 #>  6 TH_6           0.362   4.81e- 1 1.33e 2 #>  7 TH_7          -0.171   1.35e- 1 7.89e 1 #>  8 TH_8          -0.0314  2.69e- 1 8.57e 2 #>  9 TH_9          -0.163   2.78e- 1 1.71e 2 #> 10 TH10          -0.23    1.88e- 1 8.17e 1 #> 11 TH11           0.00539 1.98e- 2 3.67e 2 #> 12 TH12           0.2     1.90e+74 9.50e76 fetch_condn(lst2) #> [1] 23.30882

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: common pattern parse many .lst files model development workflow: Since fetch_all() uses purrr::safely() internally, failed runs produce NULL elements rather stopping pipeline.
code: library(purrr)  files <- list.files("models/", pattern = "\\.lst$", full.names = TRUE)  results <- map(files, \(f) {   lst <- read_lst_file(f)   fetch_all(lst) })  # Combine all THETA tables with run identifier all_thetas <- map_dfr(results, "thetas", .id = "run")  # Extract OFV for model comparison ofvs <- map_dbl(results, "ofv")

title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: lstparsR automatically detects estimation method .lst file header. following methods supported: First Order (FO) First Order Conditional Estimation (FOCE) First Order Conditional Estimation Interaction (FOCE-) Stochastic Approximation Expectation-Maximization (SAEM) Importance Sampling (IMP) Importance Sampling Assisted Mode Posteriori (IMPMAP) Markov Chain Monte Carlo Bayesian Analysis

title: lstparsR — Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: Parses THETA, OMEGA (diagonal), SIGMA (diagonal) estimates Extracts standard errors, relative standard errors, ETA shrinkage Reports objective function value (OFV) condition number Handles multi-line parameter blocks (number THETAs/ETAs) Returns NA gracefully covariance step absent run failed – safe use automated pipelines Supports FOCE-, FOCE, FO, SAEM, IMP, IMPMAP, Bayesian methods Includes interactive Shiny app point--click exploration

title: lstparsR — Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files
text: NONMEM run converge covariance step skipped, lstparsR returns NA unavailable quantities instead raising errors. critical automated workflows (e.g., pyDARWIN, PsN) hundreds runs parsed :
code: lst_fail <- read_lst_file("failed_run.lst") fetch_ofv(lst_fail)       # NA or fallback footer value fetch_condn(lst_fail)     # NA fetch_all(lst_fail)       # thetas/etas/sigmas = NULL, ofv/condn = NA

title: Extract the Objective Function Value (OFV) from a NONMEM Listing File — fetch_ofv
text: #OBJV:***...***  <value>  ***...*** — standard NONMEM 7.x banner line MINIMUM VALUE OBJECTIVE FUNCTION  =  <value> — -line format OBJECTIVE FUNCTION VALUE WITHOUT CONSTANT:  <value> — multi-line FOCE-FINAL VALUE OBJECTIVE FUNCTION  <value> — alternative wording OFV = <value> — pyDARWIN / workflow tool footer multiple #OBJV: lines present (multiple estimation steps), last occurrence returned (final step result). Returns NA_real_ warning() OFV can found — never stops.

what: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files for Population PK/PD Workflows — lstparsR-package
title: lstparsR: Tidy Parsing of NONMEM Listing Files for Population PK/PD Workflows — lstparsR-package
text: Provides functions read, clean, reshape NONMEM listing (.lst) files tidy data frames downstream population pharmacokinetic pharmacodynamic (PK/PD) analyses. Parsers extract THETA, OMEGA, SIGMA, objective function value (OFV), condition number, shrinkage values NONMEM output files. runs without covariance step handled gracefully, returning NA unavailable quantities rather raising errors.

title: Launch the lstparsR Shiny Application — run_app
lstparsR 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. read_lst_file(): reads NONMEM .lst files; returns S3 class "lst". fetch_thetas(): THETA estimates SE RSE. fetch_etas(): OMEGA diagonal SE, RSE, ETA shrinkage. fetch_sigmas(): SIGMA diagonal SE RSE. fetch_ofv(): objective function value fallback workflow footer. fetch_condn(): condition number eigenvalues. fetch_all(): convenience wrapper returning . run_app(): interactive Shiny application uploading, viewing, downloading parsed results. Supports FOCE-, FOCE, FO, SAEM, IMP, IMPMAP, Bayesian estimation methods. Handles multi-line parameter blocks (>12 THETAs spanning multiple rows). Graceful NA returns quantities covariance step absent. Validated 7,600+ real NONMEM listing files.
